---
title: "NV Control Framework"
page-layout: full
toc: true
---

# NV Control Framework

## Modular experimental control software for diamond NV center experiments

---

## Overview

The **NV Control Framework** is an object-oriented Python platform for controlling and automating experiments based on nitrogen-vacancy (NV) centers in diamond.

The framework provides a unified architecture for integrating laboratory hardware, experiment logic, graphical interfaces, and data acquisition into a scalable and maintainable software ecosystem.

---

## Why This Framework?

Modern quantum sensing experiments require the coordinated control of lasers, microwave sources, pulse generators, scientific cameras, motion stages, power supplies, and data acquisition systems.

As laboratories evolve, software often becomes tightly coupled to specific hardware, making maintenance and expansion increasingly difficult.

The NV Control Framework addresses these challenges through:

- Modular hardware abstraction
- Reusable experiment classes
- Instrument-independent APIs
- Scalable software architecture
- Automated experiment workflows
- Future-ready design

---

## Current Capabilities

| Module | Status |
|---------|:------:|
| Hardware Abstraction | ✅ |
| ODMR Measurements | ✅ |
| Pulse Sequence Control | ✅ |
| Scientific Cameras | ✅ |
| Motion Stages | ✅ |
| Power Supplies | ✅ |
| GUI Framework | 🚧 |
| Data Management | 🚧 |
| Quantum Machines OPX | Planned |

---

## Supported Hardware

| Instrument | Status |
|------------|:------:|
| Andor Neo Camera | ✅ |
| IDS Event Camera | ✅ |
| SG384 Microwave Source | ✅ |
| Swabian Pulse Streamer | ✅ |
| SmarAct XYZ Stages | ✅ |
| Keysight Power Supplies | ✅ |
| Quantum Machines OPX | 🚧 |

---

## Software Philosophy

The framework follows a layered architecture:

```
Graphical User Interface
            │
Experiment Manager
            │
Experiment Classes
            │
Hardware Manager
            │
Instrument Drivers
```

Each layer has a clearly defined responsibility, allowing new hardware and experiments to be added with minimal changes to the rest of the framework.

---

## Architecture Diagram

*An interactive software architecture diagram will be added in the next development sprint.*

---

## Development Roadmap

| Version | Planned Features |
|----------|------------------|
| v0.1 | ODMR |
| v0.2 | Rabi |
| v0.3 | Ramsey |
| v0.4 | T₁ / T₂ |
| v0.5 | Live Imaging |
| v0.6 | Quantum Machines OPX |
| v1.0 | Stable Public Release |

---

## Open Source Development

The NV Control Framework is developed following modern software engineering principles including:

- Modular design
- Version control with Git
- Continuous development
- Automated testing
- Hardware abstraction
- Documentation-driven development

The long-term objective is to create a reusable software platform for quantum sensing laboratories.

---

## Related Pages

- [Research Portfolio](../index.qmd)
- [Software](../../pages/software.qmd)
- [Research](../../pages/research.qmd)
